_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-27R Ibuprofen (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-27R: Ibuprofen — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
contraindications: Pregnancy (third trimester — risk of premature closure of ductus arteriosus; generally avoid in all trimesters unless authorized by online MC)
Known NSAID or aspirin hypersensitivity (including aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease)
precautions: Ask about ibuprofen use within the past 6 hours — avoid repeat dosing within 6 hours
Caution with GI ulcer history, liver disease
Concomitant aspirin use: ibuprofen may interfere with aspirin's antiplatelet effect
notes: Pediatric >6 months: per MI-MEDIC or weight/age-based table; standard pediatric liquid: 100 mg/5 mL
Do not give if ibuprofen was taken within the past 6 hours

dosing.notes: Do not give if taken within 6 hours; avoid in pregnancy

dosing.dose: Per MI-MEDIC or weight/age-based dosing table
dosing.notes: Standard pediatric suspension: 100 mg/5 mL; do not give if taken within 6 hours; contraindicated <6 months
